sin.notes: Exact via Euler path. Im(exp(ix)) = sin(x) for all real x.

cos.notes: Exact via Euler path. Re(exp(ix)) = cos(x) for all real x.

sinh.notes: Exact algebraic construction via exp + recip (EDL) + sub + div.

cosh.notes: Exact. Note: add costs 11 EML nodes — cosh is heavier than sinh.

tanh.notes: Numerically stable form. Node count for (exp(2x)-1)/(exp(2x)+1).

sech.notes: Exact: 1/cosh(x). Node count includes full cosh construction.

erf.notes: Best-known 5-node CBEST approximation. MSE ≈ 2e-3 over [-3,3]. Max absolute error ≈ 1.5e-2 near x=1. Use math.erf() for precision.

fresnel_s_integrand.notes: Exact: Im(exp(i*pi*x^2/2)) = sin(pi*x^2/2). This is the Fresnel integrand, NOT the Fresnel S integral. 2 nodes treating x^2 as compound.

fresnel_c_integrand.notes: Exact: Re(exp(i*pi*x^2/2)) = cos(pi*x^2/2). Fresnel C integrand.

fresnel_s.notes: Integrand is exact (2 CBEST nodes); integral via scipy or quadrature. Max error reflects quadrature fallback (scipy gives machine precision).

fresnel_c.notes: Integrand is exact; integral via scipy or trapezoidal quadrature.

j0.notes: Best-known complex-EML construction: 7 nodes at MSE < 1e-4. Run complex_mcts_search(scipy.special.j0, ...) to reproduce the formula. j0_cb() uses scipy for exact evaluation when available.

airy_ai.notes: Best-known construction: 9 nodes, MSE ≈ 2e-3. ai_cb() uses scipy for exact evaluation when available.

lgamma.notes: Stirling series with recurrence for x < 7. ~12 BEST nodes for the series.

digamma.notes: Numerical derivative of lgamma_cb. 14 nodes = 2 × lgamma nodes + sub/div.
